behaviors.description: The review input is scoped to the lines that changed, not the full content of each modified file.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is between local modules (diff extraction -> review-input builder). The behavior text is about scoping logic, not real HTTP/OS effects and not model quality. MOCK-MISS applies because this can fail when modules hand off changed-line ranges incorrectly. Rejected System: behavior text does not require real network/API semantics, only correct local scoping. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no dependence on LLM output quality or multi-agentic chaining in this behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the real diff parser and input-construction modules together using fixture PR diffs plus full file blobs; assert the produced review payload includes only changed hunks/lines and excludes unchanged file regions.

behaviors.description: Inline comments are attached to the correct positions in the diff view on the pull request.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE apply: correct diff-view anchoring depends on real GitHub PR comment positioning semantics over real HTTP. In-process mocks can accept malformed position/line mappings that GitHub would reject or render on wrong lines. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: behavior is placement correctness, not LLM quality or multi-agentic sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Run against a real test PR in GitHub: post inline comments using the automation, then fetch PR review comments and verify each comment is anchored to the expected file and diff line in the Files Changed view.

behaviors.description: The agent identifies at least one real defect in a pull request that contains a known introduced bug.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP applies directly: correctness depends on whether the real model flags an actual bug. Stubbed/model-mocked outputs would always pass and hide true detection failure. Rejected Workflow: behavior requires one detection outcome, not multiple real agentic steps chained. Rejected System as minimum: real network/PR plumbing is not the core failure artifact here; model judgment quality is.
behaviors.test_description: Use a PR fixture (or real PR) containing a deliberately introduced bug with known ground truth; invoke the real model-backed reviewer and assert at least one finding correctly targets that bug category/location.

behaviors.description: Every finding listed in the top-level summary corresponds to an inline comment that was also posted — no findings appear in the summary without a matching inline comment.
behaviors.justification: Failure is a local contract/state consistency issue across modules (finding aggregator, inline-post result tracking, summary composer). It can be reproduced with module wiring and controlled API responses. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no mandatory real OS/network dependency to detect mismatch, (b) no model-quality dependency, (c) no multi-agentic chain requirement. Rejected System: behavior text focuses on correspondence invariant, not environment-specific behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Execute reviewer orchestration with a fake PR client that records inline-post successes/failures; assert summary generation includes only findings with successful inline post records and excludes any unposted finding.

behaviors.description: The full sequence — reading the diff, posting inline comments, posting the summary comment — completes correctly for a real pull request without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires a real pull request and end-to-end execution over external APIs, so REAL-INFRA makes System the minimum. Rejected Agentic as minimum: behavior text checks completion of sequence on a real PR, not quality/non-deterministic correctness of model outputs. Rejected Workflow: text does not require multiple real agentic steps; it specifies operational completion of pipeline stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run the automation end-to-end against a real repository PR in CI: fetch live diff, create inline comments, create top-level summary, and verify all API operations succeed and artifacts exist on the PR without human retries.
